name: angular-ui-patterns
description: Modern Angular UI patterns for loading states, error handling, and data display. Use when building UI components, handling async data, or managing component states.
qualityReason: Clear use cases and focused patterns
capabilities: Implement loading states that show only when no data exists, preventing UI flash
Create error handling hierarchies from inline validation to full error screens
Build empty states with clear calls-to-action for collections and lists
Apply progressive disclosure patterns using Angular @defer for performance
Design button states with proper disabled states and loading indicators during async operations
Structure dialog and modal patterns with proper lifecycle management
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing Angular UI pattern guidance. All 63 static analysis findings are false positives: URLs are documentation references (not network calls), backticks are markdown code fencing (not shell execution), and crypto detections are text pattern misidentifications. No executable code, network requests, or command execution present.
valueStatement: Creating reliable Angular interfaces requires consistent handling of loading states, errors, and empty states. This skill provides battle-tested patterns that ensure users always understand what is happening in your application.
userTitle: Build Robust Angular UIs with Proven Patterns
limitations: Patterns are specific to Angular 17+ with modern control flow syntax (@if, @for, @defer)
Requires Angular Signals and standalone components for full implementation
Does not cover CSS styling frameworks - focuses on component logic patterns
Examples assume TypeScript and reactive programming familiarity

useCases.title: Frontend Developer Building Data-Driven Dashboards
useCases.description: Implement consistent loading, error, and empty states across dashboard widgets that fetch data from multiple APIs with varying latencies.

useCases.title: Team Lead Establishing UI Standards
useCases.description: Define organization-wide patterns for state management, error handling, and user feedback to ensure consistent user experience across teams.

useCases.title: AI Assistant Generating Angular Components
useCases.description: Guide Claude or Codex to generate Angular components that follow proven patterns for state handling, avoiding common pitfalls like silent errors or missing loading states.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Loading State Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Angular component that displays a list of items. Show a skeleton loader when loading with no data, an empty state when the list is empty, an error state with retry when loading fails, and the actual data when available. Use Angular Signals and modern control flow.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a basic data display component

promptTemplates.title: Form with Validation and Submission States
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an Angular form component with reactive forms validation. Show inline error messages for invalid fields, disable the submit button during form submission, display a loading indicator on the button, and show success or error toast notifications after the async operation completes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating user input forms with proper feedback

promptTemplates.title: Progressive Content Loading with @defer
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement an Angular page that loads critical content immediately and defers non-critical sections like comments, related items, or analytics using @defer. Include placeholder, loading, and error states for each deferred block with appropriate minimum loading times.
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing perceived performance for content-heavy pages

promptTemplates.title: Confirmation Dialog with Optimistic Updates
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a delete confirmation dialog pattern that shows a modal asking for confirmation, applies optimistic UI updates when confirmed, rolls back changes if the server request fails, and displays appropriate feedback to the user throughout the process.
promptTemplates.scenario: Handling destructive actions with safety and feedback

bestPractices: Always show errors to users - never silently catch exceptions without feedback
Display loading indicators only when there is no existing data to prevent content flash
Disable action buttons during async operations to prevent duplicate submissions
antiPatterns: Showing spinner while cached data exists - causes unnecessary visual flash during refetch
Swallowing errors in catch blocks without informing the user of failures
Allowing multiple rapid clicks on submit buttons by not disabling during processing

faq.question: When should I use a skeleton loader versus a spinner?
faq.answer: Use skeleton loaders for initial page loads and list layouts where the content shape is known. Use spinners for modal actions, button submissions, and inline operations where the content shape is unknown or the area is small.

faq.question: How do I handle errors when I have partial data available?
faq.answer: Show the existing data and display an error banner at the top or bottom of the affected section. This graceful degradation approach keeps the UI functional while informing users about partial failures.

faq.question: What is the minimum loading time for spinners?
faq.answer: Use a minimum of 200ms for loading states to prevent flickering. If an operation completes faster than 200ms, still show the loading state for the full duration to avoid jarring visual transitions.

faq.question: How should I handle form validation errors?
faq.answer: Show validation errors inline next to the affected fields only after the user has touched the field. Use clear, actionable messages that explain what is wrong and how to fix it.

faq.question: When should I use optimistic updates?
faq.answer: Use optimistic updates for non-critical actions where a rollback is acceptable, such as likes, follows, or non-destructive edits. Always implement rollback logic in case the server request fails.

faq.question: How do I make loading states accessible?
faq.answer: Use aria-live regions to announce loading state changes to screen readers. Ensure focus is managed appropriately after state changes, and provide text alternatives for visual loading indicators.
